1998-03-01.title: A Burst of Renewal Sweeps Old Beijing Into the Dumpsters
1998-03-01.abstract: Much of Beijing is being cleared by real-estate developers to make way for high-rise apartments, office buildings and shopping centers; familiar syndrome of urban renewal, long delayed by China's isolation and poverty, is unfolding fast, changing face of ancient city; single-story compounds and narrow alleys that made up much of Beijing are nearly all scheduled for demolition--their condition deemed beyond repair and their use of space too extravagant for modernizing metropolis; photos of artist Deng Haifan's paintings of life in old Beijing (M)

1998-03-01.title: Kohl's Rival Faces a Vote That's Make Or Break
1998-03-01.abstract: Election in Lower Saxony, Germany, on March 1 will decide whether Premier Gerhard Schroder keeps his majority in state legislature for opposition Social Democrats; commentators say if Schroder fares well, result will strengthen his bid to challenge Chancellor Helmut Kohl in Sept national elections (M)

1998-03-01.title: Americans Abroad Learning Studies Now Offer a Bargain
1998-03-01.abstract: Number of Americans studying abroad has jumped 84 percent over last decade; with average bill for private schools in US now more than $20,000 a year--and costs elsewhere in world much lower--growing number of American families are finding that experience that once was largely restricted to well-to-do is turning into something that middle class can no longer afford to ignore; number of students seeking bargain educations abroad has grown so quickly that backlash has been seen, both at American schools and those in foreign countries; University of Pennsylvania charges students going abroad same amount they would pay on Philadelphia campus; some countries that heavily subsidize higher education have taken to charging foreigners full price; graph (M)

1998-03-01.title: New Shape To Triangle Tying U.S. And Koreas
1998-03-01.abstract: Some South Korean politicians say that US recognition of North Korea would help stabilize Korean Peninsula; US appears committed to its policy of applying economic sanctions against North Korea and limiting official contacts (M)

1998-03-01.title: British Officer Said to Give NATO Plans to Bosnian Serbs
1998-03-01.abstract: British Government is expected to charge Army Maj Milos Stankovic with providing classified NATO information and documents to Bosnian Serb leadership during last two years of Balkan war; senior Western diplomats say he seriously undermined effectiveness of United Nations peacekeeping effort in Bosnia (M)

1998-03-02.title: Hindu Still Proud of Role In Killing the Father of India
1998-03-02.abstract: Interview with Gopal Godse, Hindu nationalist who recounts his role in plotting assassination of Mohandas K Gandhi in 1948 because of his evenhandad attitude toward Muslims; says he is proud of his role and feels vidicated by current elections returns, which point to best showing by Hindus ever in Indian national elections; map; photo (M)

1998-03-02.title: Isla de los Estados Journal; Dream Fulfilled at End of the World
1998-03-02.abstract: Andre Bronner, French navigator, fulfills childhood dream by building lighthouse on Isla de los Estados, bleak island in Cape Horn off Argentine coast, that is replica of 1884 structure that inspired Jules Verne's last novel, The Lighthouse at the End of the World; maps; photo (M)

1998-03-02.title: Iraqi Diplomat and U.N. Expert Differ Sharply on Terms of Pact
1998-03-02.abstract: Richard Butler, chief of UN weapons inspection team, clashes with Nizar Hamdoon, Iraq's envoy to UN, over critical part of recent arms inspection pact reached with Sec Gen Kofi Annan that helped avert American military action; Hamdoon, in CNN interview, says Iraq expects inspection teams at eight presidential sites to be subordinate to diplomats appointed by Annan to accompany them, but Butler says agreement clearly gives him overall responsibility for new monitoring arrangements; photo (M)

1998-03-02.title: Israel Seeks Deal to Quit Buffer Zone In Lebanon
1998-03-02.abstract: Israeli Government sends out diplomatic feelers to end increasingly costly military involvement in southern Lebanon; Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu says Government is ready to comply with UN resolution calling on it to withdraw, if Lebanon cooperates in insuring security along border (M)

1998-03-02.title: Charges of Incompetence and Laxity Are Leveled at Fragmented Forces : Belgian Police Face Growing Distrust
1998-03-02.abstract: BRUSSELS—  Passengers leaving the Anderlecht Metro station in Brussels on a recent evening were subjected to an unusually stringent ticket control.

1998-03-03.title: U.N. REBUFFS U.S. ON THREAT TO IRAQ IF IT BREAKS PACT
1998-03-03.abstract: UN Security Council unanimously endorses Sec Gen Kofi Annan's agreement with Iraq on weapons inspections, but without authorizing automatic military response of Baghdad fails to comply; majority of members--led by China, France and Russia--rebuff US and Britain by holding out against any explicit threat, particularly wording that would appear to give green light to American attack in event Iraq violates agreement; Pres Clinton reiterates threat of American military action, warning Iraq against blocking inspectors from carrying out their work; says unanimous vote sends clearest possible signal that Iraq must make good on commitment to give unfettered access to weapons inspectors; US Amb Bill Richardson says resolution reinforces US policy, and does not bar military strikes, which US believes it already has power to carry out (M)

1998-03-03.title: Early Results in India Show Gains for Hindu Nationalists
1998-03-03.abstract: Incomplete returns from India's general election indicates alliance led by Bharatiya Janata Party, Hindu nationalist group, has easily outpolled alliance led by Congress Party, but apparently not by enough seats to assure nationalists outright Parliamentary majority; photo (M)

1998-03-03.title: Serbia's Police Crush Protest By Kosovo's Ethnic Albanians
1998-03-03.abstract: Serbian police uses truncheons, water cannon and tear gas to disperse estimated 30,000 ethnic Albanians during march in Pristina, Kosovo Province, to protest recent killing of more than 20 civilians by paramilitaries; serious unrest in province, where 90 percent of people are ethnic Albanians, erupted on February 27 when four policemen were killed in ambush by rebels from Kosovo Liberation Army; map (M)

1998-03-03.title: Mondale, Clinton's 2d Envoy, Urges Suharto to Press Reforms
1998-03-03.abstract: Former Vice Pres Walter F Mondale, following meeting with Indonesian Pres Suharto, says he urged him to press ahead with painful economic reforms demanded by the International Monetary Fund to solve country's economic crisis; says they discussed current efforts to restore stability and economic growth to Indonesia and steps needed to restore confidence; is visiting Jakarta as emissary of Clinton Administration; photo (M)

1998-03-03.title: Atom Chief Of Russia Is Removed From Post
1998-03-03.abstract: Viktor N Mikhailov, Russia's powerful and pugnacious Atomic Energy Minister, is abruptly relieved of duties in move that has startled Russian and Western experts; Kremlin says Mikhailov was ssked to leave to concentrate more on scientific research; explanation is discounted by Western officials, who speculate action may be related to disputes over nuclear cooperation with Iran or suspicions of corruption in connection with sale of uranium to US (M)

1998-03-04.title: Strikes by German City Workers Frame Political Battle to Come
1998-03-04.abstract: Tens of thousands of German municipal workers stage brief strikes to bolster union demands in wage talks; at issue is future of costly social guarantees that Germans take for granted (M)

1998-03-04.title: Vienna to Go Ahead With Holocaust Shrine
1998-03-04.abstract: Mayor Michael Haupl says Vienna will go ahead with plan to build Holocaust memorial designed by Rachel Whiteread; plan stirred controversy in small Jewish community because memorial will be placed above excavations of medieval synagogue (M)

1998-03-04.title: U.S. Warns of Force If Iraq Breaks Word
1998-03-04.abstract: The day after the United Nations Security Council stopped short of endorsing an explicit military threat against Iraq, the Clinton Administration contended that the United States has the authority to use force if Iraq does not comply with United Nations weapons inspections.    President Clinton said the Council's resolution warning of ''severest consequences'' if Iraq defied the agreement sent ''a clear message.''

1998-03-04.title: U.S. Is Linking Aid to Jakarta To Its Reforms
1998-03-04.abstract: Walter F Mondale, Pres Clinton's special envoy, fails to win commitment from Indonesian Pres Suharto that he will end resistance to economic reforms that are part of $43 billion International Monetary Fund bailout; Deputy Treasury Sec Lawrence H Summers tells Senate subcommittee that Clinton will not support added IMF emergency aid unless Suharto makes 'adequate progress' toward reforms he agreed to in Jan; senior officials say Clinton signals Suharto will not be allowed to ignore accord, despite concerns about impact of aid cutoff (M)

1998-03-04.title: Congress In China: No Teeth, Strong Jaws
1998-03-04.abstract: National People's Congress, annual rite that brings together some 3,000 delegates from all parts of China, is set to open, Beijing; is expected to formally elect Zhu Rongji as Prime Minister; he has begun forcing through wary Communist Party plan to cut bureaucracy, including 4 million Government jobs; Congress, which ratifies decisions made in secret by party leaders, has evolved into more active body, with year-round professional staff helping to iron out details of new laws; photo (M)

1998-03-05.title: Ex-Nazi Is Seized; Accused of Gunning Down 500 at Maidanek
1998-03-05.abstract: German prosecutors arrest suspected Nazi war criminal accused of killing 500 people at Maidanek death camp in Poland in 1943; refuse to identify suspect and decline to confirm news reports that his name is Alfons Goetzfried (S)

1998-03-05.title: Shanghai Journal; Behind a Great Wall of Reticence, Some Sex Toys
1998-03-05.abstract: Liu Dalin opens Shanghai Sex Museum, where he displays his private collection of several thousand sex-related historical objects; contends that China should be more open about sexual matters; photo (M)

1998-03-05.title: Gregoris Staktopoulos, 88; Imprisoned in Death of George Polk, a Reporter in Greece
1998-03-05.abstract: Gregoris Staktopoulos, Greek journalist who spent decade in prison in connection with murder of George Polk, distinguished CBS news correspondent, in northern Greece in 1948, dies at age 88 (M)

1998-03-05.title: Excommunicated Priest Is Reunited With Vatican
1998-03-05.abstract: Vatican found Sri Lankan Priest Tissa Balasuriya guilty of heresy and excommunicated him in 1997, but he has been reunited with his church and excommunication order has been lifted (M)

1998-03-05.title: After a Battle, Israel President Is Re-elected
1998-03-05.abstract: Israeli Parliament re-elects Ezer Weizman to second five-year term as President after unexpectedly charged campaign for largely ceremonial office; Weizman beats back challenge by Likud party candidate Shaul Amor; photo (M)

1998-03-06.title: Advertisers Find a Formula They Like in Auto Racing
1998-03-06.abstract: LONDON—  They are sleek and fast. They travel the globe from Melbourne to Monte Carlo, surrounded by models and princes, actors and corporate moguls, and watched by some 460 million avid television spectators. And there are only 22 in each race.

1998-03-06.title: Indonesia's Crisis Deepens Over Reform Rift
1998-03-06.abstract: Indonesian crisis deepens as rupiah plummets 10 percent on concern that International Monetary Fund would suspend $3 billion loan due on Mar 15 unless Pres Suharto stops backtracking on promised economic reforms (S)

1998-03-06.title: Gun Battles in Serbia Raise Fear of 'Another Bosnia'
1998-03-06.abstract: Serbian police and paramilitary units unleash helicopter gunships and armored personnel carriers against armed Albanian separatists in Kosovo Province, forcing dozens of Serbian families and Albanian women and children to leave and cordoning off scores of villages to keep outsiders away; Serbian interior ministry says 20 Albanians and 2 Serbian policemen are killed; spokesman for Kosovo's Albanian political leader, Ibrahim Rugova, says houses were burned and massacres were carried out, proving brutality of Belgrade regime; photo; map (M)

1998-03-06.title: France Limits Legislative Inquiry on Rwanda Role
1998-03-06.abstract: Communist and Green members of French Parliament pass resolution demanding full parliamentary investigation of French role in Rwanda; want probe of how French military aid to Hutu-dominated Government there in early 1990's may have contributed to killing of hundreds of thousands of Tutsi and moderate Hutu in 1994; decision to hold only limited parliamentary inquiry into matter is called 'diversionary maneuver' by medical charity Doctors Without Borders; group says France's reputation would be at stake if questions about its role in situation are not cleared up (M)

1998-03-06.title: Hindu Bloc Moves Closer To Power
1998-03-06.abstract: India's main Hindu nationalist party grows increasingly confident that it will be able to form country's new government; election results show neck-and-neck finish between two rival politial blocs, one an alliance led by Hindu nationalist Bharatiya Janata Party and other a loose combination of Congress party and alliance known as United Front; but Congress Party leadership meeting breaks up in disagreement over whether bid for power is viable, or even desirable (M)

1998-03-07.title: I.M.F. Delays Indonesian Aid; Reforms Seem to Be Stalled
1998-03-07.abstract: International Montary Fund delays release of $3 billion in aid for Indonesia until at least Apr, citing Pres Suharto's failure to meet basic conditions for aid; Min Marie Muhammad earlier warned that any delay would hurt investor confidence and slow recovery of currency (M)

1998-03-07.title: Serbs Pound Kosovo To Repel Separatists
1998-03-07.abstract: Serbian policemen cracked down on a separatist movement in the province of Kosovo yesterday, firing mortar rounds at villages, setting fire to homes and appearing to shoot at men indiscriminately, according to those who fled the zone.     The attacks apparently left many dead in the province, where ethnic Albanians constitute 90 percent of the population. Many of the Albanians view the Serbian police and military as an army of occupation.

1998-03-07.title: Tamil Guerrillas in Sri Lanka: Deadly and Armed to the Teeth
1998-03-07.abstract: Ability of Sri Lanka's Tamil Tiger separatist guerrillas to carry out suicide bombings like recent attack in Colombo that killed 36 people reflects their remarkable success in acquiring explosives and weapons from around third world; Tigers' military procurement practices show how easy it is to find weapons, pay for them with money moved through major banks and move arms across borders; highlight how ill prepared governments are to deal with light weapons traffic; map (L)

1998-03-07.title: Spin Control:Opel and VW In a Flip Flap
1998-03-07.abstract: FRANKFURT—  The "elk test" is turning the German auto industry upside down.

1998-03-07.title: Publisher Apologizes to Hong Kong Chief for Canceled Book
1998-03-07.abstract: HarperCollins, in highly unusual move, apologizes for suggesting its decision to cancel publication of book East and West, by Chris Patten, former Hong Kong Governor, was spurred by book's shortcomings; agrees to pay Patten unspecified sum; apology is public embarrasment for Rupert Murdoch, whose News Corp owns publisher; he ordered cancellation out of concern book would jeopardize his relationship with Chinese leaders; backs Eddie Bell, HarperCollins executive chairman (M)

1998-03-08.title: Albright Warns Serbs on Kosovo Violence
1998-03-08.abstract: United States Secretary of State Madeleine K Albright is on official two-day visit to Italy; blames Pres Slobodan Milosevic of Yugoslavia for most of bloodshed in Kosovo and says that US is 'not going to stand by and watch' Serbian authorities crush political freedoms in province; on Iran, Albright says that Washington is looking for 'a pattern of action' that would demonstrate, over time, that Iran is moderating its behavior; Albright meets with Pope John Paul II at Vatican, meeting that focuses on his visit to Cuba in January; photo of Albright with Pope (L)

1998-03-08.title: Africans Resentful as Asia Rakes In Aid
1998-03-08.abstract: Africans nations are seen resentful of what they perceive as international loan agencies' swift response to Asian nation's distressed economies, contrasting Asian rescue effort with what appears to many Africans as stingy, demanding and never-ending program of life support for Africa; example of Ivory Coast cited; Ivory Coast has just been granted $2 billion in new loans, but to get there, Govt negotiated for nine months, finally agreeing to stinging measures that give uncomfortable impression that economic policy would henceforth be made at fund's headquarters in Washington; Ivory Coast Finance Min Niamien Ngoran says 'One can easily get the impression of a double standard'; World Bank and IMF officials say size and speed of their response to crisis in Asian countries is justified by importance of their economies to global financial system; map (L)

1998-03-08.title: Women See Key Gains Since Talks In Beijing
1998-03-08.abstract: Representatives from women's organizations from around world are meeting at United Nations for two weeks to take stock of progress two and half years after United Nations international conference on women in Beijing put forward proposals for govt action on women's rights; even skeptics say they are impressed at extent to which many govts have followed through on their pledges; they attribute this largely to pressure from increasingly strong local and international women's orgns (M)

1998-03-08.title: Senators Keep Gay Nominee for Luxembourg Post in Limbo
1998-03-08.abstract: Pres Clinton's nomination last year of James C Hormel, who is gay, to be ambassador to Luxembourg appears hopelessly stalled on Capitol Hill; Hormel is 65-year-old philanthropist and heir to Hormel meat-processing fortune; group of conservative Senate Republicans has placed hold on his nomination, and Sen Trent Lott, Senate majority leader, says he has no plans to bring nomination to vote (M)

1998-03-08.title: Serbs Claim Victory Over a Separatist Militia
1998-03-08.abstract: Senior Serbian officials and Western diplomats say that sweep in central Kosovo province by hundreds of Serbian police and paramilitary units appears to have failed to capture or kill most of ethnic Albanian rebels operating in area; action, which threatens to ignite another major conflict in Balkans, has left scores of people dead and dozens of houses burned (M)

1998-03-09.title: Ravaged Kosovo Village Tells of a Nightmare of Death
1998-03-09.abstract: Three-day Serbian assault on Prekaz, stronghold of ethnic Albanian separatists in Kosovo province, ends with Belgrade claiming victory; at least 26 ethnic Albanians and 2 police officers die, but death toll may be much higher; devastation described; map; photo (M)

1998-03-09.title: Cry of Muslim Women for Equal Rights Is Rising
1998-03-09.abstract: Women's rights groups throughout Islamic world are increasingly speaking out against what they call Muslim system of apartheid, where texts of Koran and appendages attached in Middle Ages are invoked to deny equal rights to women; issue of how women are viewed is at heart of much conflict in Muslim nations, from fundamenalist policies in Afghanistan and Iran, to demands to abolish discrimination in family laws in Morocco and Tunisia; activists in Morocco, Tunisia and Algeria--where women rank among more emancipated in Arab world--say UN international women's conference in Beijing in 1995 was important catalyst for defining issues and planning action; say support has grown because women are caught in growing contradictions and shifts within Islamic societies; situation in Morocco--where women still need permission from male relative to marry, name children or work and suffer other forms of discrimination--discussed; photos (M)

1998-03-09.title: A Suicide Tape on TV Inflames the Issue in Spain
1998-03-09.abstract: National debate to legalize assisted suicide in Spain is stirred by broadcast of videotape of suicide of 55-year-old quadriplegic Ramon Sampedro by television station; Sampedro, who was cause celebre in struggle to legalize procedure became quadriplegic in accident 29 years ago; had fought unsucessfully in courts for years for right to be helped to die (M)

1998-03-09.title: Ethnic Chinese Try to Keep Out of Harm's Way
1998-03-09.abstract: JAKARTA—  Amid the puddles and crumbling walls of Jakarta's morning market is the city's Chinatown, a labyrinth of shops, warehouses and narrow alleys navigated by stooped old women.

1998-03-09.title: Albright Tours Europe to Whip Up Resolve to Punish Serbia
1998-03-09.abstract: Foreign ministers of US, France and Germany agree on need for firm and clear measures to stop violence in Serbian province of Kosovo before it spreads to other nations; Sec of State Madeleine K Albright wants mix of diplomacy and punitive sanctions in dealing with Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic from six members of contact group that monitor former Yugoslavia, but Russia remains opposed and France noncommital; British and Germans are expected to support Americans, with Italians edging closer to French; Albright says Belgrade must understand seriousness of situation in Kosovo province, where Serbs have cracked down extremely harshly on independence movements by ethnic Albanians, who make up 90 percent of population; says regime in Belgrade should pay price for damage it has already done (M)

1998-03-10.title: On a Garage Floor in Kosovo, A Gruesome Serbian Harvest
1998-03-10.abstract: Serbian police release bodies of 51 ethnic Albanians killed in crackdown on outlawed Kosovo Liberation Army, bringing known death toll in fighting to 77; victims include 25 women and small children; most are from Prekaz, rebel stronghold, and include rebel commander, Adem Jashari and over 20 family members; Serbs claim rebels killed women and children to keep them from surrendering; mounting evidence indicates unarmed civilians, and apparently all males detained by police, were executed, often in front of their wives and children; photo; map (M)

1998-03-10.title: An Ex-Bosnian Serb Commander Admits Rape of Muslims in War
1998-03-10.abstract: Dragoljub Kurnarac, former Bosnian Serb paramilitary commander, pleads guilty before internation Yugoslav war crimes tribunal, Hague, to raping Muslim women during Bosnian war in 1992; is first person to plead guilty to rape as war crime; pleads not guilty to several other charges; plea surprises prosecutors (M)

1998-03-10.title: New Tinderbox, Same Fuse: A Serb Comes Full Circle
1998-03-10.abstract: Analysis of Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic's drive to crush budding uprising by ethnic Albanian majority in Serbia's Kosovo province; discusses threat that violence could knindle wider Balkan war; Milosevic has used Kosovo issue before, beginning with his 1987 transformation into Serbian nationalist leader (M)

1998-03-10.title: Crisis Aside, What Pains Indonesia Is the Humiliation
1998-03-10.abstract: Pres Suharto, wrangling with International Monetary Fund over terms of economic rescue plan, reflects Indonesians' resentment of being humiliated by IMF; part of conflict seems to stem from IMF pressure for overhaul of economic system, while Suharto apparently wants quick fix to bail out his wealthy and influential family and friends; IMF deal attacks many of their vested interests (M)

1998-03-10.title: Seoul Leader, Ex-Inmate Himself, Is Slow to Free Political Prisoners
1998-03-10.abstract: South Korean continues to detain hundreds of political prisoners, including accused spies, labor activists and leftists; Pres Kim Dae Jung, former prisoner, remains deeply suspicious of Communists and is reluctant to expend his political capital by rapidly freeing political prisoners; pledges to release them gradually; photo (M)

1998-03-11.title: Hindu Party in India Chosen for Coalition
1998-03-11.abstract: The President of India asked the main Hindu nationalist party yesterday to form a coalition Government, stirring fears among many Indians, particularly the Muslim minority, that the party would dismantle the country's historical commitment to equality for people of all faiths.     The nationalists, the Bharatiya Janata Party, now seem likely to cobble together enough votes to form a parliamentary majority after a tumultuous national election that ended on Saturday with no clear victor.

1998-03-11.title: 6 Iraqis Who Aided C.I.A. Are Ordered Deported From U.S.
1998-03-11.abstract: Six Iraqis who worked in concert with Central Intelligence Agency in failed plots against Saddam Hussein have been declared threats to American national security in court ruling so secret their lawyers cannot read it; ruling by Federal Judge D D Sitgraves means that they may be deported to their homeland, where they would probably face execution; Iraqis are Ali Yasin Mohammed Karim, Safa al-Betat, Adil Hadi Awadh, Mohammed Jawer al-Ammary, Mohammed Tuma and Ali Saleh (M)

1998-03-11.title: Hindu Party Asked to Form A Government For India
1998-03-11.abstract: Pres K R Narayanan asks Hindu nationalist party to form coalition Government in India, stirring fears among many Indians, particularly Muslim minority, that party will dismantle country's historic commitment to equality for people of all faiths (M)

1998-03-11.title: Immigrants Cast Shadow Over the Danish Election
1998-03-11.abstract: Danish voters go to polls in general election on March 11; issue that has put Prime Min Poul Nyrup Rasmussen on defensive is same one that Pia Kjaersgaard, head of small right-wing Danish Peoples Party, has made her centerpiece: immigration (M)

1998-03-11.title: At Pivotal Time, Suharto Gets a 7th Term
1998-03-11.abstract: Pres Suharto takes his oath of office for seventh time, calling on Indonesians to pull together in this time of economic hardship; he is locked in confrontation with International Monetary Fund, which is demanding that he dismantle Indonesia's system of monopolies and favoritism in return for $40 billion rescue package; photo (M)

1998-03-12.title: CLINTON MOVES UP HIS TRIP TO CHINA TO LATE IN JUNE
1998-03-12.abstract: Clinton Administration decides to move up Pres Clinton's trip to China to late June, from Nov, in effort to avoid any deterioration of relations between US and China over human rights and other issues; senior Administration officials say that Chinese officials are enthusiastic over proposal for earlier meeting; rescheduled trip is likely to follow on heels of Paula Jones sexual misconduct trial against Clinton, which is to start on May 27 in Little Rock; Administration officials say trial has nothing to do with rescheduling China trip (M)

1998-03-12.title: Tighten Belts, Suharto Tells His People, It's All Over
1998-03-12.abstract: Pres Suharto calls on Indonesians to tighten their belts, saying rapid growth in prosperity they have enjoyed over last 25 years is gone for good; it is his strongest public statement about economic crisis that has made almost all of Indonesia's 200 million people suddenly poorer; Suharto begins his seventh five-year term as President; assembly endorse B J Habibie as new Vice President; photo (M)

1998-03-12.title: Russia Vows To Push Pact Cutting Arms
1998-03-12.abstract: Prime Min Viktor S Chernomyrdin says Russia will push to ratify second strategic arms reduction treaty in order to prepare way for Pres Clinton to visit Moscow for summit meeting by middle of year (M)

1998-03-12.title: Paris Journal; Beyond the Grave, DNA Haunts Yves Montand
1998-03-12.abstract: There is sense of outrage across France after authorities dig up body of actor Yves Montand from Paris cemetery and take it to laboratory for DNA tests to settle claim of Aurore Drossard, 22, that she is his unacknowledged child; while Montand was alive, he declined to submit to blood tests ordered in paternity suit brought by Anne-Gilberte Drossard on behalf of her daughter; appeal by his family, including two heirs to fortune said to be about $3.7 million, produced court order last fall to open his tomb for DNA test; photos (M)

1998-03-12.title: As Thousands Protest, Pinochet Assumes Chilean Senate Seat
1998-03-12.abstract: Gen Augusto Pinochet, Chile's former dictator, assumes much-disputed Senate seat that protects him from prosecution for crimes committed during his 17 years in power; thousands of people demonstrate against him (M)

1998-03-13.title: Swedish Outlet Chain Gambles It Can Change Consumer Tastes : Will Cheap Chic Win Over Stylish French?
1998-03-13.abstract: PARIS—  "Paris, here we are!" gushed gigantic billboards as the Swedish cut-price clothing firm, H&M, brought its wear for the first time to a global fashion capital.

1998-03-13.title: With the Way to Power Open, Hindu Nationalists Falter
1998-03-13.abstract: India's quest for new government falters when main Hindu nationalist party falls into dispute with major ally, leaving door open for possible rival bid for power by Congress Party; in a sequence that exasperates many Indians, Jayalalitha Jayaram, former movie actress facing investigation in $1 billion fraud scandal, emerges as spoiler of what had seemed only 48 hours ago to be almost certain ascent to power by Hindu nationalists; her case reviewed; photo of Atal Bihari Vajpayee, man nominated by nationalists to be Prime Min (M)

1998-03-13.title: Kosovo Leader Urges Resistance, but to Violence
1998-03-13.abstract: Ibrahim Rugova, President of self-declared Republic of Kosovo, who comes close to being revered among ethnic Albanian majority in Pristina, Serbia, comments on situation; says he is passionately committed to nonviolence; map (M)

1998-03-13.title: Manuel Pineiro, Spymaster For Castro, Is Dead at 63
1998-03-13.abstract: Manuel Pineiro, ruthless but urbane spymaster who for more than 30 years led Cuba's intelligence apparatus and directed its efforts to export revolution to third world, dies in car crash in Havana; photo (M)

1998-03-13.title: Annan Tries to Untangle U.N. Dues From Abortion
1998-03-13.abstract: United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan asks United States House Republican leaders to justify their decision to hold up payment of back dues to United Nations until Administration agrees to provision cutting off aid to groups that support abortion (M)

1998-03-14.title: New York's Specialty Shops Bounce Back With Trendy Lines
1998-03-14.abstract: NEW YORK—  If you find yourself in New York in need of an Armani suit, chances are you'll hit one of the many department stores uptown.

1998-03-14.title: Lerik Journal; Yogurt? Caucasus Centenarians 'Never Eat It'
1998-03-14.abstract: Lerik, Azerbaijan, produces astonishing number of centenarians despite poverty, poor medical care and ignorance; residents work very hard and eat little, mostly vegetables, fruit and sour cheeses, not yogurt; most researchers credit clean, stress-free living and genetics; photos; map (m)

1998-03-14.title: Senate Urges U.N. to Try Iraqi as a War Criminal
1998-03-14.abstract: Senate votes 93-0 to urge creation of United Nations tribunal to try Iraqi Pres Saddam Hussein as war criminal; Sen Arlen Specter, resolution's main sponsor, comments; House approved similar resolution in Nov (M)

1998-03-14.title: Kim's Decree Benefits Millions; Some Dissidents Are Left Out : South Korea Grants Sweeping Amnesty
1998-03-14.abstract: SEOUL—  President Kim Dae Jung granted amnesty to more than 5.5 million of his countrymen on Friday — for everything from traffic tickets to spying — and freed 2,304 people from jail, including some of the nation's best-known political prisoners.

1998-03-14.title: Longtime Dissident Still Held by Seoul
1998-03-14.abstract: Woo Yong Gak, North Korean who may be world's longest-serving political prisoner, is not included in South Korea's amnesty of traffic offenders and prisoners; amnesty touched 5.5 million people, mostly by erasing records of speeding tickets, and led to release of 74 people regarded by human rights groups as political prisoners; Woo, held since 1958, has refused to renounce belief in Communism and North Korean Government; photos (S)

1998-03-15.title: Clinton May Veto Funds Bill on Abortion Issue
1998-03-15.abstract: Pres Clinton scores Republican efforts to slip unrelated controversial provisions into emergency spending bills, radio speech; hints he may veto bill if Congress attaches ban on financial aid to groups that offer abortion services abroad; scores Republican efforts to bar Federal Communications Commission from ordering free broadcast time for political candidates (M)/

1998-03-15.title: Indian Congress Party Picks Sonia Gandhi
1998-03-15.abstract: India's Congress Party elects Sonia Gandhi president, re-affirming family dynasty that has long governed party; she scores Hindu nationalists as 'threat' to secular ideals of independence leaders, speech; Hindu nationalists, after days of uncertainty, win support of Jayalalitha Jayaram, whose control of 27 parliamentary seats makes her key to their efforts to win parliamentary majority; she accompanies letter of support with fierce denunciation of nationalist leaders (M)

1998-03-15.title: A Quebecer Trumpets The Case For Canada
1998-03-15.abstract: Interview with Min Stephane Dion, bilingual professor who is Canada's official crusader for national unity, chosen in 1996 by Prime Min Jean Chretien to stand up to Quebec separatists; he notes how his views differ from those of late father, Leon, considered intellectual leader of Quebec's search for identity (M)

1998-03-15.title: World Bank Vs. Reformers on How to Still Feed Russia When It's 64
1998-03-15.abstract: World Bank forces Russia's most ardent reformers to scale back their plan to convert state-run pension system into a program of private individual retirement accounts; thought plan is too risky and pressed for an incremental approach that would leave state-financed pension system with a major role; critics say World Bank dogmatically imposed its vision of a paternalistic state role in social welfare; dispute illustrates enormous influence of World Bank and International Monetary Fund over Russia's social and economic policy and also shows that World Bank's role in encouraging social change is more complex--and more contentious--than generally realized; graph (M)

1998-03-15.title: China Keeps Murdoch And His TV On Hiatus
1998-03-15.abstract: China continues to keep its media market closed to Rupert Murdoch even though he has curried favor when Chinese leadership for 10 years; Murdoch has invested over $1 billion in Star TV, Asian satellite television network that is intended to be New Corp's route to China, but winning approval to broadcast, collecting subscriptions fees and selling advertising are proving difficult; New Corp points to Star TV's partial ownership of Phoenix Satellite Television Co of Hong Kong as an important is modest sign of progress (M)

1998-03-16.title: AMERICAN TOPICS : California Bar Owner Becomes Butt Of the State's No-Smoking Statute
1998-03-16.abstract: A bar owner in Auburn, California, has been convicted of two infractions for allowing patrons to light up in violation of the new state anti-smoking law. It was apparently the first such trial under the law that bans smoking in more than 35,000 bars and casinos. The owner, Bill Ostrander, was fined a total of $300

1998-03-16.title: Official's Arrest Puzzles South Africa
1998-03-16.abstract: Robert McBride, former guerrilla for African National Congress, is arrested in Mozambique during effort to purchase weapons; visit to Mozambique remains mystery; African National Congress says his visit was not authorized and that McBride was acting on his own; says it is not involved in arms deal (M)

1998-03-16.title: French Leftists Surge in Regional Elections
1998-03-16.abstract: Exit polls suggest leftist parties of French Prime Min Lionel Jospin's nine-month-old Government coalition will defeat conservative opposition in about half of country's 22 regional assemblies in light voting (M)

1998-03-16.title: Bill to Push Africa Trade Is Approved
1998-03-16.abstract: US House of Representatives approves broad trade bill promoting private US investment in 48 countries of sub-Saharan Africa, some among poorest countries in world, that will give them preferential treatment if they move toward democracy and free-market economies; bill allows duty-free and quota-free exports to US for 10 years, encourages creation of free-trade zones and creates position in office of US trade representative to focus on African issues; other provisions noted; proponents say it underscores fundamental shift in American relations with Africa that favors trade over aid; measure still needs Senate approval (M)

1998-03-16.title: Israel Calls British Aide's Visit To Disputed Site Unacceptable
1998-03-16.abstract: Israeli Government criticizes as unacceptable intention of British Foreign Sec Robin Cook to visit site of disputed new Jewish settlement in eastern Jerusalem; Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu may refuse to meet him; planned visit is part of Cook's three-day visit to Middle East as representative of European Union (M)

1998-03-17.title: Briton Settles Dispute Over Visit to Israeli Site
1998-03-17.abstract: Israel resolves dispute with Britain over planned visit of Foreign Sec Robin Cook to site of controversial new Jewish neighborhood in eastern Jerusalem by agreeing that Cook will travel there with Israeli, rather than Palestinian officials (M)

1998-03-17.title: Do Sanctions Really Work? Debate Is On In Burma
1998-03-17.abstract: RANGOON—  Burma's economy, squeezed for years by Western sanctions and weakened by the economic crisis affecting much of Asia, is in tatters.

1998-03-17.title: Armenians Turn Out To Elect New Leader
1998-03-17.abstract: Armenians turn out in large numbers to vote in presidential election that Western political analysts say could profoundly affect stability in oil-producing Caucasus region; 12 candidates are competing in contest (S)

1998-03-17.title: Indonesia's New Cabinet Takes Office
1998-03-17.abstract: New nationalist-oriented Cabinet takes office in Indonesia; faces tough negotiations with international lenders over how best to solve deepening economic crisis (S)

1998-03-17.title: Spain Expands Inquiry Into Argentine State Terror
1998-03-17.abstract: Names of 9,000 people, many of Spaniards and their decendants who disappeared during military rule in Argentine from 1976 to 1983, are presented to Spanish judge in Madrid, Spain, sharply widening his inquiry into Argentine-sponsored terrorism (S)

1998-03-18.title: Sudan Hinders U.N. Aid as South Faces Famine
1998-03-18.abstract: United Nations aid officials say people of Adet, Sudan, are victims of campaign by Islamic Government to starve people in rebel-held territories because they support guerrillas fighting for autonomous south; Government has banned relief flights to all but four airstrips in region; UN officials say ban has crippled relief operation at time when people have little food left in storage after two years of drought; say food is being stockpiled in places like Adet, while children in neighboring towns are beginning to starve; map; photo (M)

1998-03-18.title: Proficiency of English Declines as Schools Switch to Teaching It as a Second Language : Tongue-Tied in Hong Kong
1998-03-18.abstract: HONG KONG—  A recent telephone call to a major stock brokerage, asking when the manager would be available, produced a confusing reply: "He will call you after the phone."

1998-03-18.title: Netanyahu Angrily Cancels Dinner With Visiting Briton
1998-03-18.abstract: Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu cancels dinner with British Foreign Sec Robin Cook, charging that visiting official had violated agreement by meeting with Palestinians at planned site of disputed Jewish settlement in Jerusalem; photo (M)

1998-03-18.title: Senate Panel Tries to Get I.M.F. to Tighten Its Lending Process
1998-03-18.abstract: Senate Appropriations Committee approves $18 billion in financing for International Monetary Fund, but only if fund formally votes to make major changes in way it lends money to nations in distress; Treasury Sec Robert E Rubin and some Republicans immediately express strong opposition (M)

1998-03-18.title: As Czech Skinheads Escalate Attacks, Gypsies Start to Put Up a Fight
1998-03-18.abstract: PRAGUE—  Helena Bihariova, 26, did not deserve to die on the night of Feb. 17. Her only crime was to be a dark-skinned Czech citizen of Gypsy origin in the northern Czech town of Vrchlabi.

1998-03-19.title: Illegal Trade in Homemade Computers Thrives : Beijing's 'Backyard' Industry
1998-03-19.abstract: BEIJING—  Swinging their truncheons, a dozen "intellectual property police" strode into action along Beijing's Zhongguancun street. At the sight of them, the hawkers of pirated compact discs and CD-Roms hurriedly gathered their wares and decamped.

1998-03-19.title: Senate Struggles to Pay Attention to the Remapping of NATO
1998-03-19.abstract: Senate takes up issue of NATO expansion as afterthought, debating it for second day in row only after chaotic scheduling short-circuits debate on education; Sen John Warner, who is leading opposition to expansion, criticizes Senate for its lack of interest in foreign policy (M)

1998-03-19.title: Despite U.N. Role, Serbs Find No Peace in Croatia
1998-03-19.abstract: Some $450 million in international aid and two years of United Nations administration fail to turn Vukovar, Croatia, into an example of how minority ethnic population can be successfully integrated into one of nationalist states in former Yugoslavia; of 120,000 ethnic Serbs who lived in eastern Slavonian enclave two years ago, nearly half have fled; there have been dozens of assaults and three ethnic Serbs have been killed by local Croatians, who assumed control on enclave in Jan from United Nations; map (M)

1998-03-19.title: French Jobless Find the World Is Harsher
1998-03-19.abstract: First of two articles, Help Unwanted, on unemployment in France and the pressure on Government to reverse the escalation in jobless benefits; pattern of worker entitlement, protest and Government capitulation is deep-rooted in France and has swollen welfare state into one of most comprehensive and costly in Europe, with much of the burden borne by employers; employers say payroll taxes and benefits are main reasons why the are not creating jobs; French economy is expected to post healthy 3 percent gain in 1998, but it will not produce many new jobs; graph; photo (M)

1998-03-19.title: U.S. Military Draws Up Battle Plans on How to Win an 'Info War'
1998-03-19.abstract: PARIS—  Information warfare was born with hackers, anarchistic whiz-kids who got so bored waiting for their turn running a big IBM mainframe at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology that they turned to pranks such as taking a piano apart, then reassembling it atop the chapel spire.

1998-03-20.title: 'Iron Ring' Around Russia? Comment Provokes Outburst
1998-03-20.abstract: Debate in United States Senate over NATO expansion provokes Sen Joseph Biden to erupt in protest when Sens John Warner and Daniel Patrick Moynihan object to redrawing NATO's boundaries to admit Hungary, Poland and Czech Republic, and others in later rounds, including Baltic states, that would form 'iron ring' around Russia; Biden's protesting remarks about 'iron ring' noted (S)

1998-03-20.title: Indonesia-I.M.F. Talks Show Flexibility on Ending Impasse
1998-03-20.abstract: Indonesian officials begin meetings with International Monetary Fund officials, with both sides offering signals of flexibility on deadlock that has caused halt in disbursements of international aid; officials seek to deal with Indonesia's huge burden of corporate debt, problem that IMF had not previously addressed; photo (M)

1998-03-20.title: NEW CHINA LEADER PROMISES REFORMS FOR EVERY SECTOR
1998-03-20.abstract: Chinese Prime Min Zhu Rongji lays down ambitious benchmarks for sweeping social change, pledging to make ailing state-owned industries solvent, overhaul country's banking system and redesign Govt, cutting its staff by half--all within three years; but zeal for economic revolution that Zhu displays at his first news conf as leader of Govt is not matched by any hint of rapid political change; he flatly rules out an official reappraisal of 1989 military crackdown on pro-democracy demonstrators in Tiananmen Square, which killed hundreds and has left lingering wound in society; Zhu says that subsidized housing--a hallowed benefit for country's workers--will be phased out entirely; photo (M)

1998-03-20.title: Oil-Price Drop Threatens to Squelch Russia's Economic Rebound
1998-03-20.abstract: Russia faces new threat of falling oil prices, just as it appears that Russia appears to have weathered economic turmoil that battered Asia; decline in world pprice for oil is expected to result in billions of dollars in lost tax revenue; its is hurting Russia's major oil companies and making it harder for them to make needed investments (M)

1998-03-20.title: Handheld Computer Starts to Come of Age
1998-03-20.abstract: PARIS—  This year should see the handheld computer graduating from a costly gadget to a relatively inexpensive and productive tool.

1998-03-21.title: Lady Enniskillen, 80, Journalist and Diplomat
1998-03-21.abstract: Dowager Countess of Enniskillen, formerly Nancy MacLennon, American journalist and consular officer, dies on Feb 24 at Kinloch House, her home in Amulree, Perthshire, north of Edinburgh, at 80 (S)

1998-03-21.title: Lott Puts Off Vote On Adding to NATO
1998-03-21.abstract: An hour after President Clinton urged Senate approval of NATO's expansion, the majority leader, Trent Lott, put off action. Mr. Lott said he was frustrated by a filibuster on a bill to set up education savings accounts. Approval had been expected as early as next week for adding three nations to NATO.    Article, page A5.

1998-03-21.title: Turkish Premier Confronts Military Over Islam
1998-03-21.abstract: Turkish Prime Min Mesut Yilmaz, after surviving attempt by religious conservatives to bring down his Govt this week, is confronting an even more powerful group: the military commanders who effectively put him into office last year, and military is pushing back; Islamic-oriented members of Parliament try to topple Yilmaz after his Education Minister ordered ban on wearing of head scarves by female university students; motion fails; Yilmaz has expressed ambivalence about ban on head scarves, which military commanders view as symbols of religious fundamentalism; in recent days, military commanders have complained that Yilmaz is not fighting hard enouh against fundamentalism (M)

1998-03-21.title: Fugitive Cuban Athletes Are Said to Reach Safety
1998-03-21.abstract: Journey of four Cuban baseball players and a coach who fled their country on small boat and then seemed to vanish takes puzzling turn with swirl of vague, unconfirmed reports that they washed ashore in Dominican Republic, weakened but alive after nine days at sea; map (M)

1998-03-21.title: Europe Joins America in Golden Rush To the Market
1998-03-21.abstract: LONDON—  Whether it's New York or London, Frankfurt or Madrid, stock markets seem to go in only one direction these days.

1998-03-22.title: Blacks Seek Focus on Africa Policy
1998-03-22.abstract: Pres Clinton's coming trip to Africa reflects growing influence on foreign policy of African-Americans like Mayor Wellington E Webb of Denver, who wants US to put Africa at top of its foreign policy agenda (M)

1998-03-22.title: Rapid-Fire Inspections Planned for Iraq Sites
1998-03-22.abstract: Richard Butler, who heads United Nations commission disarming Iraq, plans rapid-fire inspections of previously off-limits Presidential properties to determine which will get spot inspections later--with much less notice; insists that this is only beginning of this phase of inspection process; there is speculation among diplomats that once the UN team has visited the eight Presidential sites, Iraq will argue that this phase of arms investigation is over and that country can move closer to a lifting of sanctions (M)

1998-03-22.title: For Iraq, U.N. Plans Rapid-Fire Searches
1998-03-22.abstract: With another crucial test looming between Iraq and the United Nations, the head of the commission disarming Iraq said he plans rapid-fire inspections of previously off-limits Presidential properties. That will determine which buildings will get spot inspections later, with less notice. And the new procedures could set off a new conflict.    Article, page 8.

1998-03-22.title: Africa Enduring Rebirth Awaits Clinton's Arrival
1998-03-22.abstract: Pres Clinton's 12-day trip to Africa will emphasize positive changes that have taken place in recent years; military rule is giving way to democracy, South African apartheid is gone and reforms have bolstered a continent-wide economic expansion; Clinton will seek to persuade American audience that Africa not only exists but matters and to impress on Africa that it and US must pursue a relationship marked by mutual respect, shared economic interests and deep veins of common history; map (M)

1998-03-22.title: Changing Fortunes: Hubris and Humility as U.S. Waxes and Asia Wanes
1998-03-22.abstract: Nicholas D Kristof analysis on the confounding of the expectation that was widely held in Japan and elsewhere in Asia just a few years ago that their economies were ascendant while America was in decline; says the kind of hubris and triumphalism that blinded Asians to their problems may well do the same to US; says that when things are going well, one is always liable to credit the billiance of one's economic and social structure (M)

1998-03-23.title: Silent Hotels / Building Boom Goes Bust : 5-Star Luxury at Bargain Prices
1998-03-23.abstract: SURABAJA, Indonesia—  Welcome to the executive suite at the five-star Hotel Majapahit, a celebration of marble, Oriental rugs and gold plate.

1998-03-23.title: Belfast Journal; The Chain This Catholic Wears Speaks Volumes
1998-03-23.abstract: Interview with Alban Maginness, who became first Catholic mayor of Belfast, Northern Ireland, after Protestants lost their long control of city council last year; he cites efforts to reach out to Protestants; Sammy Wilson, Protestant councilman, says split between Protestant Unionists and Catholic Nationalists on council continues; photo (M)

1998-03-23.title: Yugoslavs Try to Outwit Albright Over Sanctions
1998-03-23.abstract: American and European officials say Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic may have made just enough concessions over conflict in Kosovo Province to confound State Sec Madeleine K Albright's efforts to intensify Western sanctions against him; Albright is set to leave for Europe to increase pressure on Milosevic to open serious talks on political autonomy with ethnic Albanians, who make up 90% of population in Serbian province; will meet, Bonn, with ministers of Britain, France, Germany, Italy and Russia, members of contact group that monitors events in former Yugoslavia (M)

1998-03-23.title: Mutilation Seen as Risk For the Girls Of Immigrants
1998-03-23.abstract: Rep Louise M Slaughter, leader in drive against female genital mutilation, makes public Health and Human Services Department analysis that found over 160,000 girls and women in immigrant communities in United States may have been victims of traditional practice or are at risk of being subjected to it; practice has been illegal in US since 1997; report is based on statistical analysis by Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (M)

1998-03-23.title: Nigerians Pin High Hopes on Pope's Visit
1998-03-23.abstract: Pope John Paul II, mixing spiritual and political in nation bracing for critical elections, beatifies Nigeria's first candidate for sainthood, Rev Cyprien Michael Iwene Tansi, and presides over Mass for some million people, Onitsha, Nigeria; repeatedly alludes to human rights situation and need for greater democracy in Nigeria; photo (M)

1998-03-24.title: 4 Who Would Succeed Chernomyrdin
1998-03-24.abstract: Brief biographical sketches of Grigory A Yavlinsky, Sergei Kiriyenko, Boris Y Nemtsov and Yuri M Luzhkov, who are often mentioned as Pres Boris N Yeltsin's next Prime Minister (M)

1998-03-24.title: Chirac Acts to Thwart Far-Right's Push for Power
1998-03-24.abstract: French Pres Jacques Chirac, in televised speech, expresses concern about offers of far-right National Front party of Jean-Marie Le Pen to help keep mainstream conservatives in power in wake of closely fought regional elections; warns against cooperating with extremists and damaging France's reputation for upholding democratic values; photos (M)

1998-03-24.title: Into Africa
1998-03-24.abstract: R W Apple analysis contends that Pres Clinton by going to Africa has demonstrated that US no longer considers it peripheral; maintains that there is widespread skepticism about Clinton's goal of establishing important economic links with Africa; says Clinton is raising expectations, perhaps beyond his capacity to fulfill them (M)

1998-03-24.title: YELTSIN DISMISSES HIS ENTIRE CABINET IN SHOW OF POWER
1998-03-24.abstract: Russian Pres Boris N Yeltsin dismisses his entire Cabinet, including his loyal and dedicated aide, Prime Min Viktor S Chernomyrdin; says dismissals are effort to make economic reforms more energetic and effective; although much is made of Yeltsin's move, there are only three major victims so far: Chernomyrdin, First Deputy Prime Min Anatoly B Chubais, controversial economic reform leader, and Interior Min Anatoly S Kulikov; most of other Cabinet members will almost certainly keep their jobs; Yeltsin names Energy Min Sergei Kiriyenko as First Deputy Prime Minister until he names permanent Prime Minister (M)

1998-03-24.title: The Reformers Did It. No, Blame the Bankers.
1998-03-24.abstract: Alessandra Stanley analysis contends that when Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin dismissed his entire Cabinet, question was not just why, but who was really behind decision; maintains that bombshell mostly appears to be messy blend of Yeltsin's will and wishes of some of his closest advisers; says that while some observers paint decision as backstage maneuver by powerful bankers seeking to consolidate their influence, economic reformers chose to view shake-up as small victory for their team; photo (M)

1998-03-25.title: Russia Reported to Agree to Sell Heavy Weapons to Yugoslavia
1998-03-25.abstract: US officials say that Russia has agreed to sell Yugoslavia tanks, attack helicopters and other weapons in move that could threaten delicate peace in Balkans; deal, which was arranged in Moscow in Dec, has not been made public, and US officials say they are in dark about details (M)

1998-03-25.title: That's the Official Name of Its Crackdown on Illegal Immigrants : Malaysia to Indonesians:Go Away
1998-03-25.abstract: KUALA LUMPU—  The Malaysian Navy has launched a small fleet. The police have descended almost nightly on squatter villages, arresting those without working papers. And trawler captains convicted of ferrying illegal immigrants have been sentenced to three years in prison and six lashes with a rattan cane.

1998-03-25.title: IN UGANDA, CLINTON EXPRESSES REGRET ON SLAVERY IN U.S.
1998-03-25.abstract: Pres Clinton, speaking to thousand children in meadow near school in Mukono, Uganda, expresses regret for role of US in African slavery, as he reels off list of errors in American policy toward continent that includes turning to ruthless dictators as allies during cold war; Clinton has been under some pressure at home to issue formal apology for slavery, but he stops well short of such explicit statement; Clinton meets with Pres Yoweri K Museveni and praises his efforts to expand Uganda's economy and educate its people; map; photos (M)

1998-03-25.title: Americans Are Warned Not to Stay at 2 Hotels Linked to Tripoli : U.S. Plays Anti-Libya Card in Prague
1998-03-25.abstract: PRAGUE—  Two of Prague's top hotels have been blacklisted by the U.S. government because they have been purchased by a company with purported links to the government of Libya, and American travelers have been warned that they could face criminal penalties if they stay at them.

1998-03-25.title: U.S. Ties Unaffected, Russian Tells Albright
1998-03-25.abstract: Foreign Min Yevgeny M Primakov tells Sec of State Madeline K Albright in Moscow that Russia's foreign policy will remain unchanged despite political turmoil; photo (M)

1998-03-26.title: A Touchy South Africa Takes Pains Not to Kowtow
1998-03-26.abstract: Pres Clinton is spending 11 days in Africa, but he is really making two different trips: one to Africa and one to South Africa; South Africa is of Africa, but different too; its economy dwarfs that of any other African countries; some 300 American companies do business there; in Nelson Mandela, South Africa has President whose international stature rivals Clinton's (M)

1998-03-26.title: Annan Is Stern But Friendly On Israel Visit
1998-03-26.abstract: United Nations Sec Gen Kofi Annan, in speech to Israel Council on Foreign Relations, accuses Israel of chain of 'provocative acts' that he says contributed to crisis in Israeli-Arab relations; says fundamental formula of Oslo agreements, land for peace, is key principle to Middle East peace

1998-03-26.title: Blacks on a Brooklyn Street: Both Cynics and Optimists Speak Out
1998-03-26.abstract: Midway through Pres Clinton's tour of six African countries, it may be too early to know how trip has affected African-Americans--Clinton's most faithful constituency; conversations with several people in Bedford-Stuyvesant, Brooklyn, reveal that Clinton's every move and word are being carefully dissected by African-Americans from all walks of life; photos (M)

1998-03-26.title: Critics Say U.S. Ignored C.I.A. Warnings of Genocide in Rwanda
1998-03-26.abstract: Former Clinton Administration officials and human rights experts say Clinton Administration ignored warnings of impending genocide in Rwanda, including Central Intelligence Agency study saying 500,000 people could die if country exploded; say even when it was clear that hundreds of thousands of Rwandans were in mortal danger, US stopped United Nations from taking action that might have saved those lives (M)

1998-03-26.title: U.S. OFFICIALS SAY MEXICAN MILITARY AIDS DRUG TRAFFIC
1998-03-26.abstract: US analysts say arrest of Mexico's drug-enforcement chief, Gen Jesus Gutierrez Rebollo, in 1997 shows much wider military involvement with drug traffickers than Mexican authorities have acknowledged; classified Drug Enforcement Administration report says arrest of Gutierrez Rebollo followed secret meetings between Mexican Army officers and country's biggest drug mafia; US officials say there is growing evidence that military officers discussed deal to let drug gang operate in exchange for huge bribes, and that some arrangement may have been in place before gang's leader, Amado Carrillo Fuentes, died after extensive plastic surgery last year; photos (M)

1998-03-27.title: Helms Vows To Make War On U.N. Court
1998-03-27.abstract: Sen Jesse Helms says he opposes permanent international criminal court proposed by Pres Clinton to deal with crimes such as frenzy of killing in Rwanda; Helms says he opposes giving United Nations 'trapping of sovereignty' that court would represent; Helms's threats come as team of American legal experts meets with representatives from 100 other nations over how court will operate (M)

1998-03-27.title: Fiery Uprising Kills 8 Detainees in Camp And a Police Officer : Crackdown In Malaysia Sets Off Riot By Illegals
1998-03-27.abstract: SEMENYIH, Malaysia—  Nine people were killed Thursday as hundreds of illegal Indonesian immigrants rebelled at a detention camp here hours before they were to be deported across the Strait of Malacca.

1998-03-27.title: Top Conservative Jumps Parties to Take On Quebec Separatists
1998-03-27.abstract: Canada's long struggle to stay united takes unusual turn as one of country's most prominent politicians, Jean Charest, switches parties so he could spearhead effort to defeat Quebec separatists in coming provincial elections; polls suggest Charest could win election against current separatist government; Liberal Party victory, bringing Charest into office as Premier, could forestall third referendum on independence for French-speaking province (M)

1998-03-27.title: St. Petersburg Journal; Russian Woman Sails Solo in a Sea of Cadets
1998-03-27.abstract: One of Russia's most prestigious military institutions, Frunze Naval Academy, is first and only naval academy in country to accept female cadet; Cadet Lyudmila Yolshina says she was not greeted with anything like hostility that drove Shannon Faulkner from The Citadel after only one week; she says she was told to report any problems to superiors, and her fellow cadets fear consequences if they harass her; mostly, she seems surrounded by amused condescension; but cadets and faculty of Frunze draw line at her dream of serving on combat ship; Russian women have extraordinary record of combat bravery in wartime, yet they are relegated to lowly support position in rear during peacetime; Yolchina was accepted at Funze only after writing letter to Pres Boris N Yeltsin and Defense Min Igor D Sergeyev, imploring them to let her continue family tradition of naval service; photos (M)

1998-03-27.title: Law Enforcers Link North Korea to Drug Trade : A Pyongyang Connection?
1998-03-27.abstract: BANGKOK—  International narcotics specialists and U.S. drug-enforcement officials are increasingly concerned that North Korea may be involved in illegal drug transactions to gain hard currency for its depleted Treasury.

1998-03-28.title: Shaolin Temple Journal; Where Zen and Kung Fu Got Off to a Flying Start
1998-03-28.abstract: Shaolin Monastery at foot of China's Song Mountain in Henan is fabled home of Zen Buddhism and of Asian martial arts; students study thousands of special martial moves there; De Yang, 32, is acclaimed master of monastery's stylized and sometimes bizarre routines of strength and self-defense; De Yang says practice of martial routines is simply physical expression of meditation exercises used in pursuit of spiritual enlightenment; monastery has become major tourist attraction in China; photo; map (M)

1998-03-28.title: Threatening Parliament, Yeltsin Names Neophyte Premier
1998-03-28.abstract: Pres Boris N Yeltsin names Sergei Kiriyenko, little-known 35-year-old technocrat, as Russia's new Prime Minister and warns that he would disband Parliament if it rejects nomination; Yeltsin stunned nation by dismissing several Cabinet members, including his longtime Prime Minister, Viktor S Chernomyrdin; Kiriyenko was plucked from obscurity to fill Chernomyrdin's post after aides told Yeltsin that he could not himself take on role of Acting Prime Minister, as he planned, because it would be unconstitutional; Yeltsin appears to be keeping field open by selecting Kiriyenko, but there is streak of recklessness to strategy; Yeltsin is not well and if he should die or become incapacitated, Kiriyenko would take over as President for three months until new elections are called (M)

1998-03-28.title: From Mandela, a Gentle Admonishment
1998-03-28.abstract: South African Pres Nelson Mandela, meeting with Pres Clinton in Cape Town, says United States, as leader of world, should reach out to its enemies and seek talk of peace; Mandela praises some Clinton policies but stoutly defends South Africa's right to remain friendly with nations US considers rogues, like Libya, Iran and Cuba; Mandela gives Clinton emotion-drenched tour of Robben Island, where he spent 18 years as political prisoner, confined to tiny cell and forced to do backbreaking labor; Clinton notes that Mandela emerged from that confinement not in anger, but in hope and determination to put things right; Mandela expresses reservations about African trade bill passed by House of Representatives that would require African nations to privatize their economies and liberalize their political systems in order to attain free access to American markets; photos; map (M)

1998-03-28.title: For Russian Premier, A Youthful Neophyte
1998-03-28.abstract: President Boris N. Yeltsin picked a little-known technocrat, only 35 years old, as Russia's Prime Minister and said he would disband Parliament if it rejected the nomination.

1998-03-28.title: India's Confident Leader Faces a Key Vote Today
1998-03-28.abstract: Prime Min Atal Bihari Vajpayee of India strengthens his week-old Hindu nationalist coalition by assuring Indians that policies will not depart from those of previous governments; nationalists are expected to have trouble holding coalition together over longer term; photo (M)

1998-03-29.title: How Cartel Tried to Buy Bank Group In Mexico
1998-03-29.abstract: Scandal surrounding efforts of late drug baron Amado Carrillo Fuentes to take over Grupo Financiero Anahuac reveals how far drug money-laundering has reached into upper echelons of Mexico's financial system; Carrillo, beginning in Mar 1995, sunk about $12.6 million into ownership position in bank, which he was already using to launder money; his drive to control bank was thwarted when regulators seized bank in Nov 1996 because big shareholder was implicated in fraud unrelated to drugs; money-laundering probe began in Apr 1997, when police found Anahuac company car at safe house used by drug cartel (M)

1998-03-29.title: Secularism And Islam
1998-03-29.abstract: Analysis of demand by Turkey's powerful military for vigorous drive against Islamic extremism; generals hold they fulfill duty to guard state and that today's greatest danger is fundamentalism (M)

1998-03-29.title: Indonesian Students Keep Protests Well Within the Pale
1998-03-29.abstract: Indonesia's normally quiet universities have begun to respond to economic crisis; students are usually careful to confine nearly daily demonstrations against deprivations to campuses, which are ringed by armed police officers; movement has remained tenative and well behaved, dominated by conservative wing that seeks to avoid confrontation and violence; map; photo (M)

1998-03-29.title: Clinton Commemorates the Struggle to End Apartheid
1998-03-29.abstract: Pres Clinton, visiting South Africa as part of 12-day African tour, marks moral courage of long struggle against apartheid, Soweto; comments on gritty, complex task of state-building; says United States aid programs should be extended; Pres Nelson Mandela elaborates on his remarks during news conference with Clinton that US helped white regime during his struggle against apartheid, television interview; photo (M)

1998-03-29.title: Israeli Arabs March Home, To a Place They Can't Live In
1998-03-29.abstract: Thousands of Israeli Arabs march, Sheik Dannun, Israel, protesting Israeli expropriation of their villages; mark anniversary of similar 1976 protest during which Israeli forces killed six Israeli Arabs; map; photo (M)

1998-03-30.title: After Clinton's Trek: Fear of a Crushing Embrace?
1998-03-30.abstract: News analysis of Pres Clinton's visit to South Africa as part of African visit; even though one of Clinton's central themes is partnership, which is widely welcomed as break with paternalism of past, he left behind major question over terms of potential partnership; US could crush comparatively small nations and economies of continent in its embrace because of its overwhelming economic and political power (M)

1998-03-30.title: ARMS CONTRACTORS SPEND TO PROMOTE AN EXPANDED NATO
1998-03-30.abstract: Six largest US military contractors who stand to gain billions of dollars in sales if Senate approves NATO expansion, have made enormous investments in lobbyists and campaign contributions to promote cause in Washington; have spent $51 billion on lobbying in past two years; their zeal, as well as extensive spadework by Clinton Administration and support by Senate Foreign Relations Committee chairman Jesse Helms, have made it virtually certain that Senate will approve treaty to admit Poles, Hungarians and Czechs; industry is also concentrating efforts abroad, setting up partnerships in Eastern Europe to give companies foothold (M)

1998-03-30.title: Oil-Rich Neighbor Eclipsing Armenia
1998-03-30.abstract: Regional political structure in oil-rich Caspian Sea basin is threatening Armenia's hold on Nagorno Karabakh, part of neighboring Azerbaijan over which it gained control in 1994; rush for unatapped reserves and billions of dollars in planned investments have loosened Russia's grip on region and turned oil-rich Azerbaijan into new hub of regional influence (M)

1998-03-30.title: Taipei Journal; The Mayor Won't Abide Licensed Licentiousness
1998-03-30.abstract: Interview with Kuan Hsui-chin, one of Tapiei's licensed prostitutes who expresses bitterness over shutdown of city's 18 legal brothels by crusading Mayor Chen Shui-bian in 1997; photo (M)

1998-03-30.title: No Ultimatum for Israel, Albright Assures U.S. Jewish Groups
1998-03-30.abstract: US Middle East negotiator Dennis Ross will return to Washington to brief Pres Clinton on reactions by Palestinian leader Yasir Arafat and Israeli Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu to new peace proposals; leaders of American Jewish groups say they have been assured by Sec of State Madeleine K Albright that US will not present Israel with ultimatum for withdrawing from West Bank (M)

1998-03-31.title: Exiled Leader's Return to Test Cambodian Democracy
1998-03-31.abstract: Norodom Ranarridh returns to Cambodia to run for re-election as Prime Minister against Hun Sen, with whom he shared power and who ousted him in coup nine months ago; political analysts say July election will amount to verdict on $2 billion international effort to plant seeds of democracy in country ravaged by wars and killings for more than 30 years, but warn that unless political violence and manipulation are brought under control, election will not be free and fair; photo (M)

1998-03-31.title: Once Seen As a Political Beacon, Senegal Backslides
1998-03-31.abstract: Senegal, once regarded as model of political progressiveness and stability under former Pres Leopold Sedar Senghor who resigned in 1980 after more than 20 years in office, has become cautionary example of cynicism; while other countries were rejecting one-party past in 1990's and organizing increasingly competitive elections, many Senegalese have complained that elections are routinely won by ruling party that buys votes or manipulates results to maintain 38-year lock on power (M)

1998-03-31.title: Yeltsin Says He Won't Run For Another Term in 2000
1998-03-31.abstract: Russian Pres Boris Yeltsin says he will not run for re-election in 2000; refrains from endorsing any of potential candidates (M)

1998-03-31.title: Post-Communist Russia Plumbs Its Soul, in Vain, for New Vision
1998-03-31.abstract: Russia is in search of national idea that can define its essence and inspire its citizens, but struggle has thus far been exercise in conflict and confusion; new national anthem has no words because nobody can agree on what it should say, politicians are still squabbling over tricolor flag and Government commission established in summer 1997 to develop national idea has come up empty-handed; even though disputes over nature of Russian soul has dominated Russian life for centuries, question of national purpose has acquired fresh urgency as nation struggles to redefine itself following collapse of Soviet empire, which provided so much of its identity and mission for 20th century; Yeltsin Government would like to have concept, slogan or sound bite to mobilize public and counter Communists, who while minority at least have an ideology, but has failed to convince public that it has compelling vision of what lies at end of country's long hard road from socialism; photo (M)

1998-03-31.title: U.S. Effort Fails To End Impasse In the Mideast
1998-03-31.abstract: US envoy Dennis B Ross concludes four-day round of shuttle diplomacy in Middle East after apparently failing to break year-old deadlock in Israeli-Palestinian peace negotiations; Prime Min Benjamin Netanyahu reportedly rejected outright key American proposal for withdrawal from 13 percent of West Bank--part of package said to also include specific actions by Palestinians to fight terrorism as well as timetable for future steps; Ross's failure appears to confront US with options of either exerting greater pressure on Netanyahu by making proposals public, or of abandoning role of mediator (M)
